title: Potato Pancake Brunch Plate
imagePrompt: A round, deeply golden potato pancake forms the base of the plate, its pressed surface crisped to an uneven, lacy-edged brown. Pale yellow soft-scrambled eggs are spooned over the top in loose, silky folds, with irregular pieces of caramelized chorizo — edges dark red and rendered — scattered across, finished with fresh green coriander leaves and thin spring onion rounds. Shot overhead on a light, bright surface — white marble or pale grey stone — in clean morning window light. Airy and fresh brunch energy: no shadow drama, just the warm plate colors against a light ground. Minimal props; the plate and its scattered ingredients speak for themselves.
subtitle: A crispy rösti-style potato pancake stacked with buttery soft scrambled eggs, chorizo, and fresh garnishes.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Serves 1–2. Scale up the potato pancake for more people.
items: 2 large russet or Yukon gold potatoes (~600g / 1.3 lbs), grated
Salt + black pepper to taste
2 tbsp neutral oil or butter (for pancake)
4 eggs
1 tbsp butter (for scramble)
100g / 3.5oz chorizo, sliced or crumbled
Handful fresh coriander (cilantro)
2 spring onions, sliced thin
Hot sauce to taste
ingredients.callout: Most important step: Squeeze all the moisture out of the grated potato before it hits the pan — it's the difference between crispy and soggy.

shoppingList.item: 2 large russet or Yukon gold potatoes

body.html: A brunch plate built around a golden, press-flat potato pancake as the base. Soft scrambled eggs and crispy chorizo go on top, finished with coriander, spring onion, and hot sauce.

items: Grate + squeeze potatoes dry, season
Press flat and fry potato pancake until golden and crispy
Cook chorizo until caramelized
Soft scramble eggs on low heat
Stack + garnish — coriander, spring onion, hot sauce

body.html: Don't skip the squeeze: The mandolin or box grater gets the potato fine enough to cook through fast, but a towel-squeeze to remove excess water is what makes it crispy.

items: Grate potatoes on the fine side of a mandolin or box grater.
Transfer to a clean kitchen towel and squeeze hard — remove as much moisture as possible.
Season with salt + pepper and toss to combine.
Don't rinse — you want the starch to help the pancake bind.

body.html: Don't fear the mandolin: Go slow, use the guard, and you'll get beautifully thin, even shreds. A box grater works too.

slides.title: Cook the Potato Pancake
slides.checkboxLabel: Fry pancake until crispy on both sides

items: Heat a skillet (carbon steel or cast iron) over medium heat.
Add oil or butter. Once shimmering, add the grated potato.
Press firmly flat with a spatula — good contact with the pan is everything.
Cook undisturbed for 5–7 minutes until deep golden underneath.
Carefully flip (wide spatula or plate-flip method) — press flat again.
Cook another 5–6 minutes until crispy and cooked through.
Transfer to a plate while you make the toppings.

body.html: Don't rush it: The crust only forms when you leave it alone. Resist checking or moving it for the first 5 minutes.

items: In the same pan (or a clean one), add chorizo slices or crumbles.
Cook over medium heat for 3–4 minutes, turning occasionally.
Cook until edges are caramelized and the fat has rendered.
Remove from pan and set aside.

slides.title: Soft Scramble the Eggs
slides.checkboxLabel: Eggs soft scrambled, silky and barely set

items: Whisk eggs with a pinch of salt.
Melt butter in a non-stick or carbon steel pan over low heat.
Add eggs and stir constantly with a spatula.
Keep stirring and folding — don't stop, don't raise the heat.
Pull off the heat while still slightly underdone (carryover cooking finishes them).
Eggs should be silky and just barely set — not dry, not watery.

body.html: Low and slow is non-negotiable. High heat = rubbery eggs. Low heat + constant stirring = silky curds.

items: Place the potato pancake on the plate as the base.
Spoon soft scrambled eggs on top.
Add chorizo pieces over the eggs.
Scatter fresh coriander leaves and sliced spring onions.
Hit it with hot sauce to finish.
Serve immediately — the pancake softens as it sits.

body.html: Avocado belongs here: A few slices between the eggs and chorizo would not be wrong. 100% a missed opportunity.
